channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Schools face challenges teaching children how to identify and handle misinformation without getting political, especially in recent days as false claims swirl surrounding the recent hurricanes and the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: A haunting Patch Tuesday for October: 117 updates (and 5 zero-day flaws)
item.description: This month’s Patch Tuesday delivers a large set of patches from Microsoft that fix 117 flaws, including five zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43573, CVE-2024-6197, CVE-2024-20659, CVE-2024-43572 and CVE-2024-43583).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Google used influence, ultimatum to cut controversial deal on California news
item.description: Google insisted on taxpayer money as part of the deal with California to help set a precedent for potential talks about payments to the news industry in other states, according to the people familiar with the talks.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: MoneyGram hack exposes customers' sensitive data, cripples services
item.description: MoneyGram, an American payment and money transfer platform, confirmed a data breach that exposed sensitive customer data to hackers, forcing its services offline for five days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Several Bay Area counties are among the most at risk in the U.S. for natural disasters. One of them is trying to improve its preparedness.
item.description: Alameda, Santa Clara and Contra Costa counties are facing the 4th, 5th and 8th highest risk within California, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Katie Lauer, The Mercury News

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting the timing right at Setterwalls to invest in AI support
item.description: In recent years, there’ve been a number of smaller AI products geared toward the legal profession, but it wasn’t until gen AI caught on that Swedish law firm Setterwalls really saw the benefit. “We saw that there was potential to benefit on a broad front,” says CIO Daniel Sandberg.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here's what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption beyond just the loss of a device.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Milton has caused thousands of flight cancellations, is yours one of them? What to know
item.description: Thousands of flights in and out of the U.S. have been canceled this week as Hurricane Milton barreled into the Gulf of Mexico and plowed across Florida.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham-Philips And David Koenig, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How a Cold War plan to stop nuclear proliferation could protect the world from an AI arms race
item.description: As Mark Twain said, “History doesn’t often repeat itself, but it does rhyme.” We are fortunate that it does, because it means we can use lessons from the past at least as a rough guide to the present.
item.dc:creator: Ash Jogalekar, Fast Company

item.title: If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow?
item.description: "General intellectual property law in the United States is still trying to figure out AI and a number of features of AI," procurement attorney Dan Ramish said. The post If your agency is buying artificial intelligence, what are you exactly buying anyhow? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Astera Labs debuts Scorpio fabric switch to accelerate PCIe for AI, cloud
item.description: There’s a new type of switch that could soon be showing up in AI-optimized data centers, a PCIe 6 fabric switch. Astera Labs this week unveiled its new Scorpio fabric switch, which it says is the industry’s first PCIe 6 switch.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI tracker charts growth in deepfakes ahead of election
item.description: The German Marshall Fund has launched a tool to track deepfakes created by artificial intelligence (AI) that are targeting elections and circulating in the U.S. and across the globe. The tool comes in a historic year for elections.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: How to use Java generics to avoid ClassCastExceptions
item.description: Generics are used in Java to allow types or methods to operate on objects of various types while providing compile-time type safety. Generics address the problem of ClassCastExceptions being thrown at runtime as a result of code that is not type safe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI says cybercriminals are using ChatGPT more to influence elections
item.description: OpenAI has seen a number of attempts where its AI models have been used to generate fake content, including long-form articles and social media comments, aimed at influencing elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a report on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab,' plans to bring autonomous driving tech to other models in 2025
item.description: Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Greene takes fire for remarks about weather
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) has again thrown herself at the center of controversy over her embrace of claims that the government and humans are “controlling” the severe weather as Hurricane Milton barrels toward Florida.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Someone reinvented the toilet so that you can do your business anywhere
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how the Generation 2 Reinvented Toilet transforms waste into safe materials, a promising sanitation solution that could transform lives and communities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KREX 5
